subject: a young elf scrambling backward on a wooden floor, thrusting a tambourine outward as an improvised weapon against an unseen threat
description: A young elf with pointed ears and a magenta headband sprawls across worn wooden floorboards, propping himself up with one hand while thrusting the other forward. In his outstretched grip is a glowing tambourine — its drumhead lit warm gold from within, jingles catching the light. He wears a green tunic with a magenta sash, a satchel at his hip glows green, and his expression is wide-eyed open-mouthed panic. The interior behind him is a broken room consumed by the outside world: a shattered or moonlit window glows blue-cyan at upper left, leafy vines and pale moths or paper-like flying creatures invade from all sides, and the floorboards are stained with spreading dark fluid (likely blood). The composition is action-frozen, mid-shot, leaning right as he leans left, with the tambourine as the brightest highlight in the frame.
foreground: elf figure on floor wielding glowing tambourine
background: broken interior overgrown with vines and moths, moonlit window
emotion: panicked, wide-eyed, mouth open in a shout or gasp; desperate defensive posture
